commit.message:
fix(cd): restore prod deployment pipeline and fix GHCR push (#1318)

* fix(cd): restore SOPS age secret and kubeconfig context in prod deploy

The CD workflow was missing two critical steps that were accidentally
removed during the v2.31.2 refactoring:

1. SOPS Age secret creation in cluster: Without the sops-age secret
   in the flux-system namespace, Flux cannot decrypt SOPS-encrypted
   secrets (*.enc.yaml files). Re-add the idempotent kubectl create
   command that ensures the secret exists before reconciliation.

2. Kubeconfig context rename: The ksail.prod.yaml expects context
   'devantler-prod' but the KUBE_CONFIG secret may use a different
   context name. Re-add the context rename step for robustness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cd): use new GHCR package path and explicit GITHUB_ACTOR

Change the OCI registry path from ghcr.io/devantler-tech/platform to
ghcr.io/devantler-tech/platform/manifests. The old path likely has
stale package permissions preventing GITHUB_TOKEN from writing. Using
a new path will auto-link the package to the repository on first push.

Also explicitly pass GITHUB_ACTOR in the push step env for robustness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: date off-by-one and single-day range display on availability page (#165)

Bug 1: formatDateMedium now anchors to noon UTC before formatting,
preventing timezone-induced date shifts when midnight-UTC timestamps
(dateRangeStart/dateRangeEnd) are displayed in western timezones.
Previously, April 12 midnight UTC would show as April 11 in LA.

Bug 2: Single-day availability requests now show one date instead of
a redundant range (e.g., 'April 12, 2026' instead of
'April 12, 2026 – April 12, 2026'). Applied to both JSX display
and email reminder code.

Scoped anchorToNoonUTC to formatDateMedium only — formatDateLong is
intentionally left unchanged since it's used for real datetime values
(event.startTime) where timezone-correct display is needed.

commit.message:
Add per-call CancellationToken to async client methods (#395)

I attest that I have verified

All async methods in `Microsoft.Teams.Api.Clients` used a single
`_cancellationToken` baked in at construction time, making per-call
cancellation impossible and shared-instance cancellation a blunt
instrument. `ReactionClient` already had the correct pattern; this
brings all other clients into alignment.

## Changes

- Added `CancellationToken cancellationToken = default` to every async
method in: `ActivityClient`, `BotSignInClient`, `BotTokenClient`,
`ConversationClient`, `MeetingClient`, `MemberClient`, `TeamClient`,
`UserTokenClient`
- Each method resolves the effective token using the same fallback
pattern already established in `ReactionClient`:

```csharp
// Before
public async Task<Resource?> CreateAsync(string conversationId, IActivity activity)
{
    var res = await _http.SendAsync(req, _cancellationToken);
    ...
}

// After
public async Task<Resource?> CreateAsync(
    string conversationId,
    IActivity activity,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
{
    var token = cancellationToken != default ? cancellationToken : _cancellationToken;
    var res = await _http.SendAsync(req, token);
    ...
}
```

- Updated `Samples.TargetedMessages` and `Samples.Reactions` to pass the
handler's `cancellationToken` to all `context.Api.*` client method
calls, using the new per-call overloads.

Fully backwards-compatible — existing callers are unaffected since the
parameter defaults to `default`.

commit.message:
Introduce message system, Lua bindings, and property parsing overhaul

* Adding message bubbling

* Add UIKit messages, Lua bindings & routing

Introduce new UIKit message types (e.g. UpdateMatrix, HitTest) and their argument structs, message ID constants, and Lua bindings. Register new metatables and push/check helpers in UIKit_export.c and expose UpdateMatrixMsgArgs/HitTestMsgArgs in the UIKit module. Add OBJ_RaiseEvent in component.c to dispatch messages according to their routing policy. Also include minor whitespace/formatting cleanups in several plugin headers (SceneKit, SpriteKit, DarkReign) and related UIKit XML/property updates.

* Add message-type support and enum helpers

Introduce message-type plumbing and enum helper macros across the codebase. Update docs/schemas/module.dtd (rename parent->same-as and add routing attr). Extend ClassDesc with MessageTypes and NumMessageTypes and expose OBJ_GetMessageTypeAtIndex in include/orca.h. Generated plugin exports now define empty MessageType arrays, set MessageTypes/NumMessageTypes in class descriptors, and add ENUM/FIELD macros to simplify enum-to-string and Lua binding code. Property headers now include GetMessageType macros and k<Class>NumMessageTypes constants; template files were updated to generate these changes.

* Update Lua bindings generation and message types

Regenerate and refactor autogenerated bindings and templates: introduce generic STRUCT macro and common helpers (read_property, write_property, parse_property) to replace per-struct boilerplate in multiple * _export.c files, add DECL/ARRAY_DECL macros, and simplify enum/property declarations to use EnumValues arrays. Change UIKit message field types from fixedString to string in the XML and update UIKit.h accordingly (new message arg structs, luaX push/check prototypes, and const char* for EventName/URL). Add/modify template files (tools/templates/*), add tools/templates/export/properties.php, add SpriteFrame IDs in SpriteKit properties, and remove obsolete libs/wasm README/.gitignore. Also apply minor formatting/whitespace adjustments in generated properties headers.

* Add flow.c; register Lua methods in exports

Add source/geometry/flow.c implementing parse_property, read_property and write_property for various PropertyType data types and register the new file in the Xcode project. Update many *_export.c files (core, filesystem, geometry, renderer, and several plugins) to resolve Lua methods from corresponding _*Methods arrays in the __index macros and add NULL sentinels to luaL_Reg tables. Move the stristr implementation into source/editor/ed_stab_object.h (remove its declaration from include/shared.h) and clean up includes (uncomment <stdbool.h>, add <stdlib.h> where needed). These changes enable proper property parsing/serialization and method lookup from Lua, and tidy up duplicated/placeholder code previously present in geometry_ops.c.

* Register additional MsgArgs and fix struct parsing

Export: add many lua_setfield registrations for additional *MsgArgs types across UIKit, core and filesystem modules so event/message argument classes are exposed to Lua (e.g. mouse/key/window/timer/visibility/creation/attach/release/etc). Templates: stop skipping event exports without fields in tools/templates/export.php so empty MsgArgs types are still emitted. Struct parsing: make the from-string parser robust by guarding the loop with 'tok &&' in tools/templates/export/structs.php to avoid reading past token end when fewer tokens are provided.

* Skip structs in fromstring; add UI text parsers

Stop attempting to parse nested struct properties in generated fromstring handlers and add text-to-struct converters for UIKit shorthands. The fromstring loop now checks tokens and skips properties whose DataType == kDataTypeStruct to avoid consuming sub-struct tokens. UIKit: added EdgeShorthand/MarginShorthand/BorderRadiusShorthand text conversion functions and override their fromstring to use the new parsers; updated XML to remove some constructor/unwrap attributes. Many property tables and headers were expanded/renamed (capitalized scalar members and added indexed/subfields) across plugins and core (SpriteKit, SceneKit, DarkReign, UIKit, core, geometry, filesystem, and others) to expose individual fields (and corresponding ID defines). Module registration calls (on_*_module_registered) were moved after lua_setfield registrations so classes/fields are available when the callbacks run. These changes improve text parsing of shorthand types and expose finer-grained properties to Lua.

* Normalize mat3/mat4 property names and IDs

Rename matrix property fields from V[0]..V[15] to V0..V15 and update their identifier hashes across geometry_export.c and geometry_properties.h. Also adjust the property name normalization in tools/model/module.php to strip square brackets in addition to dots (use array form of str_replace). These changes standardize property identifiers (removing bracketed names) and update corresponding DECL entries and #define IDs to match the new naming convention.

* Use REGISTER_CLASS macro for ClassDesc

Add a REGISTER_CLASS macro to factor the repeated ORCA_API struct ClassDesc initializers and replace many explicit ClassDesc definitions with REGISTER_CLASS(...) invocations across several export files. This reduces duplication, standardizes the ClassDesc layout (including Xmlns and property/message fields), and makes future class registrations easier to maintain. Affected files include multiple plugin and source export files and a template: plugins/DarkReign/DarkReign_export.c, plugins/SceneKit/SceneKit_export.c, plugins/SpriteKit/SpriteKit_export.c, plugins/UIKit/UIKit_export.c, source/core/core_export.c, source/filesystem/filesystem_export.c, source/geometry/geometry_export.c, source/renderer/api/renderer_export.c, and tools/templates/export/components.php.

* Change parse_property signature and return type

Update parse_property to accept a mutable struct pointer and return an int status. The declaration in multiple export files and the export template was changed from 'const void*' to 'void*'. The implementation in source/geometry/flow.c was updated from void to int, now returns TRUE/FALSE on success/failure and performs proper assignments (including string strdup/free and enum validation). This enables parse_property to modify property storage and report parsing success.

Files updated: plugins/*_export.c, source/core/core_export.c, source/filesystem/filesystem_export.c, source/geometry/flow.c, source/geometry/geometry_export.c, source/renderer/api/renderer_export.c, tools/templates/export/structs.php

* Fix metatable order in STRUCT macro: newuserdata before setmetatable

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/corepunch/orca/sessions/e632b71f-ea89-4757-ac3f-0a4a299ae334

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update core_main.c

* Fix userdata init order and property parsing

Refactor STRUCT macro across export modules to set metatable before initializing userdata (avoid comma-operator misuse) and properly memset/newuserdata order for safe initialization (plugins/*, source/* export files). Centralize property parsing by calling read_property from luaX_readProperty and stubbing the old switch logic (#if 0). Add an explicit require("orca.geometry") on core module registration. In style conversion, fetch the "TypeString" field from the PropertyType before calling it. These changes fix userdata/metatable initialization bugs and unify property-reading behavior.

* Remove intermediate and handle object properties

Return actual object/component pointers for kDataTypeObject and remove the redundant intermediate storage. property_accessors.c: PROP_GetValue now returns either the component (via CMP_GetObject when TypeString is set) or the raw object pointer; PROP_SetStateValue no longer writes to the removed intermediate field. property_internal.h: removed the intermediate member from Property. geometry/flow.c: simplify read_property handling for kDataTypeObject to accept table inputs with a __userdata field, emit an error for unsupported input types, and keep alternative string/require handling commented for future reference. These changes simplify object property handling and avoid keeping a duplicate intermediate reference.

* Use struct field offsets; rename MarginShorthand

Pass field offsets when reading/writing struct properties from Lua and when parsing property strings (use ((char*)ptr)+Offset). Update generated export code (__new/__index/__newindex/__call/fromstring) across plugins and core modules to operate on per-field value pointers. Rename MarginShorthand to Thickness throughout headers, XML, property tables and bindings, and update related accessors/IDs. Add PROP_SetDirty(api) and consolidate dirty/modified flag handling by calling it from PROP_SetStateValue and when loading property values from Lua. Also adapt geometry flow helpers to accept value pointers and prevent double-free of sentinel strings.

* Rename SendMessage2 API to MsgSend

Rename the direct message API from SendMessage2 to MsgSend across the codebase. Updated declarations and implementation (core.h, object_lua_msg.c), Lua export and binding (core_export.c), API docs (core.xml), plugin usage (share/plugins/file-xml.lua), and tests/docs (tests/*.lua, docs/testing.md) to use msgSend instead of sendMessage2. This aligns the C symbol names and Lua method name and updates all call sites accordingly.

commit.message:
Use newest Npgsql when targeting net8.0 and above (#13724)

We are currently using Npgsql 8.0 because that's the last version that's
still compatible with .NET Standard 2.0/netfx; but it seems wrong to
force an older version of Npgsql on modern .NET users just because netfx
users require it

So for the versions of the PgVector MEVD provider targeting
net8.0/net10.0, use the latest Npgsql 10.0; netstandard2.0/net462 still
use Npgsql 8.0. It's not ideal to use different versions of Npgsql
across different TFM targets of the same library, but it seems like the
lesser of the possible evils here.

This incidentally also takes care of the ReloadTypesAsync problem in
#13706.

---------

commit.message:
analysis: Claude Code 架构精读——512K LOC 中提取 10 个可迁移模式

从泄露的 Claude Code TypeScript 源码中提取 10 个生产级 Agent CLI 的工程模式：
- buildTool() 工厂 + 并发安全声明
- async generator 流式 QueryEngine 循环
- 5 阶段启动优化（pre-import→parallel→lazy→deferred→conditional）
- 三层权限模型（规则+分类器+拒绝追踪升级）
- Worker setState no-op 状态隔离
- 4 层 Skill 加载 + token 预算控制
- MEMORY.md 索引 + 按需 topic 注入
- 双层 Feature Flag（编译时 DCE + 运行时 GrowthBook）
- 35 行 Observable Store
- UUID 环形缓冲去重 Bridge 协议

衍生想法：mini_symphony 并发执行+隔离模式、Agent 断路器模式
KB 条目 28→29

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
